title: OIDC, OTP & Security Services

classes.name: OIDCService
methods: _init(): void — sets db (DB_WRITE/auth), loads this.config.providers
getProviderConfig(providerId): Promise<ProviderConfig|null> — resolves Google endpoints via discovery or uses raw config
#getGoogleDiscovery(): Promise<object|null> — lazy-fetches GOOGLE_DISCOVERY_URL, caches result
getCallbackUrlForFlow(flow): string|null — builds /auth/oidc/callback/<flow> URL; validates against VALID_OIDC_FLOWS
getAuthorizationUrl(providerId, state, flow): Promise<string|null> — assembles OAuth2 authorize URL with URLSearchParams
signState(payload): string — jwt.sign(payload, jwt_secret, {expiresIn: 600})
verifyState(token): object|null — jwt.verify(token, jwt_secret)
exchangeCodeForTokens(providerId, code, redirectUri): Promise<object|null> — POST to token_endpoint with authorization_code grant
getUserInfo(providerId, accessToken): Promise<object|null> — GET userinfo_endpoint with Bearer token
findUserByProviderSub(providerId, providerSub): Promise<User|null> — SELECT from user_oidc_providers, delegates to get-user service
linkProviderToUser(userId, providerId, providerSub, refreshToken?): Promise<void> — INSERT into user_oidc_providers (handles UNIQUE conflict)
createUserFromOIDC(providerId, claims): Promise<OutcomeObject> — delegates to signup service, calls linkProviderToUser on success
getEnabledProviderIds(): Promise<string[]> — filters providers by valid config

classes.name: OTPService
methods: create_secret(label): {url, secret} — creates TOTP (SHA1, 6 digits, issuer puter.com), returns otpauth URL + secret
create_recovery_code(): string — crypto.randomBytes(6) → base32 encode → trim to 8 chars
verify(label, secret, code): boolean — validates TOTP token with delta in [-1,0,1]
gen_otp_secret_(): string — crypto.randomBytes(15) → base32 encode → trim to 24 chars (private)

classes.name: AntiCSRFService
methods: _construct(): void — initializes map_session_to_tokens = {}
'__on_install.routes'(): void — registers GET /get-anticsrf-token endpoint (auth2 required, subdomain guard)
create_token(session): string — creates/gets CircularQueue(10) per session, pushes new token, returns it
consume_token(session, token): boolean — calls CircularQueue.maybe_consume(token)
generate_token_(): string — crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex') (private)
classes.route: GET /get-anticsrf-token — returns {token} for authenticated user; blocks api. subdomain

classes.name: SUService
methods: _construct(): void — initializes sys_user_ and sys_actor_ as TeePromise instances
'__on_boot.consolidation'(): Promise<void> — fetches system user via get-user service, creates Actor(UserActorType), resolves both promises
get_system_user(): Promise<User> — returns sys_user_ promise
get_system_actor(): Promise<Actor> — returns sys_actor_ promise
sudo(actor?, callback): Promise<T> — runs callback in Context.sub({actor, user}) with elevated privileges; defaults to sys_actor_ if no actor given

classes.name: VerifiedGroupService
methods: _init(): void — subscribes to 'user.email-confirmed' event; on fire: fetches user, removes from default_temp_group, adds to default_user_group

classes.name: CircularQueue
classes.note: Fixed-size queue (capacity 10 for CSRF tokens); used by AntiCSRFService
methods: push(item)
maybe_consume(item): boolean

classes.name: Actor
classes.note: Represents an authenticated principal; has a type (e.g. UserActorType)
methods: Actor.adapt(actor): Actor — static factory/coerce

relationships: AntiCSRFService composes CircularQueue (per-session token store, capacity 10)
SUService uses Context (sub-context for privilege escalation)
key_patterns: All services extend BaseService; lifecycle hooks: _construct() → _init() → __on_boot.*  → __on_install.*
OIDCService: OAuth2 authorization_code flow with JWT-signed state for CSRF; supports Google (discovery) + generic OIDC providers
OTPService: TOTP (RFC 6238) with 6-digit SHA1, window delta [-1,0,1]; base32-encoded secrets/recovery codes via hi-base32
AntiCSRFService: session-keyed CircularQueue(10) of random 32-byte hex tokens; consumed once (maybe_consume); route blocked on api. subdomain
SUService: privilege escalation via Context.sub({actor, user}); system actor resolved once at boot.consolidation phase
VerifiedGroupService: event-driven group promotion on email confirmation (temp → verified group)
database_tables: user_oidc_providers (user_id, provider, provider_sub, refresh_token) — managed by OIDCService
important_notes: Module styles mixed: OIDCService/SUService use ES module (import/export); OTPService/AntiCSRFService/VerifiedGroupService use CommonJS (require/module.exports). All extend BaseService which extends concepts.Service from @heyputer/putility.
